cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer. The question is asking what we can infer from the Toy Fair 2014 article. First, let me read through the article again to pick out the key points. The article mentions that the 2014 Toy Fair had a trend called "Larger Than Life," where everything is really big and out there. They talk about big toys with big play value. Also, there was a push towards education with STEAM (science, technology, engineering, art, math), including word games and high-tech games. They mention 3D games and robots as trends. Looking at the options: A) Toys are becoming more educational than fun. The article does say there's a push towards education with STEAM, but it's not clear if they are becoming more educational *than* fun. The article still mentions fun aspects like high-tech robots and games. So maybe A isn't the best choice. B) Robots are great favorites of buyers. The article says they saw some super cool, high-tech games and mentions robots, but does it say they are great favorites? Maybe robots are part of the trend, but the article doesn't specifically state that buyers favored them the most. So B might not be the best answer. C) Big toys will be popular this year. The article definitely emphasizes the "Larger Than Life" trend, with Adrienne Appell saying "everything is really big and really out there." So this seems like a solid inference. The "Larger Than Life" trend suggests big toys are a major trend for 2014. D) Simple card games will disappear on the market. The article mentions that simple card games were shown at the fair, but it doesn't say they will disappear. In fact, they might still be part of the market. So D is probably incorrect.
